title: GameStop Earnings Volatility and Meme Stock Price Reactions
text: GameStop shares experienced sharp intraday volatility after activity on Keith Gill's (Roaring Kitty) account, with the stock surging as much as 13% before quickly reversing gains. The posts appeared and then disappeared from his social media account, with the posts deleted around 5:40 p.m. in New York, less than an hour after they went up. GameStop's stock experienced a burst of meme-stock volatility, trading nearly 13% higher before reversing sharply overnight, driven by social media-fueled excitement and capital plans proposals. GameStop stock traded over 4% lower overnight, late Monday following the initial spike. Volatility persists, with the stock capable of sharp intraday swings on news or social media sentiment. However, market watchers note that GameStop's trading patterns have become somewhat more normal in 2026, with fewer extreme intraday swings compared with previous years, which while reducing spectacular upside potential, has also lowered downside risk. GameStop reported stronger-than-expected fiscal fourth-quarter 2025 results in late March, posting adjusted earnings per share of $0.49 that beat estimates.
